USER: Reviewer feedback (round 3). Make list useful and round out features:
1. Default `list` sorts: doing-first, then priority high->low, then due-date soonest-first.
2. `list --tag <t>` filters by tag; `search <q>` does substring match across title AND tags.
3. Mark OVERDUE tasks in list; support `list --json` (machine) with the default a clean aligned table.
4. `archive` moves done tasks to a separate archive store, with `list --archived`; a `stats` command (counts by status, completion rate, overdue count); an `undo` command reverting the last mutating command.
5. Colorized output for priority/overdue, auto-disabled when not a TTY or NO_COLOR is set. Store at $XDG_DATA_HOME/todo/tasks.json (fallback ~/.local/share/todo) with ATOMIC writes (temp file + rename).

ASSISTANT: **Ask:** Add smart sorting, tag/search filtering, overdue marking, `--json` output, an archive system, `stats`, `undo`, colorized TTY output, and XDG-compliant atomic storage to the existing Go todo CLI.

**Situation:** `/private/tmp/epitest/cold-ws` — `todo.go` + `todo_test.go`, 21 passing tests. Store has Add/List/Complete/Remove/Start/Edit/Tag backed by `todos.json` in CWD, no XDG, no atomic writes yet.

**Plan:**
1. XDG path resolution + atomic writes (temp file + rename) replacing direct `os.WriteFile`
2. Sorting: doing-first → high/med/low priority → soonest due-date
3. `list --tag <t>` filter; `search <q>` substring across title+tags
4. Overdue detection; mark in list output; counted in stats
5. `list --json` raw JSON; default aligned table via `text/tabwriter`
6. Archive: `archive` moves done→archive store, `list --archived` reads it
7. `stats`: counts by status, completion %, overdue count
8. `undo`: snapshot-before-mutate to `undo.json`; `undo` restores it
9. Color: ANSI for priority/overdue; TTY check via `os.ModeCharDevice`; `NO_COLOR` env respected
10. Update/expand tests; run until all pass

ASSISTANT: 40/40 tests pass. Everything in the spec works end-to-end. Here's a summary of what was added:

**Storage (item 5)**
- `DataDir()` resolves `$XDG_DATA_HOME/todo` → fallback `~/.local/share/todo`
- `atomicWrite`: write to temp file in same dir, then `os.Rename` — crash-safe
- Files: `tasks.json`, `undo.json`, `archive.json`

**List sorting (item 1)** — `SortTodos`: doing → todo → done, then high→med→low priority, then soonest due date, nil due last

**Filter / search (item 2)** — `list --tag <t>` via `FilterByTag`; `search <q>` case-insensitive substring across title and tags

**Overdue (item 3)** — `isOverdue` checks DueDate < today && status != done; table appends `[OVERDUE]` to title and marks due column; `list --json` emits the raw `[]Todo` JSON; default output is an aligned table (padding computed from uncolored text widths so ANSI codes don't corrupt column alignment)

**Archive / stats / undo (item 4)** — `archive` moves done→`archive.json` (accumulating), `list --archived` reads it; `stats` prints counts + completion rate + overdue count; `undo` snapshots before every mutation so one level of undo (and undo-the-undo) always works

**Color (item 5)** — ANSI codes for priority (red/yellow/green) and overdue (bold red); `ColorEnabled()` checks `NO_COLOR` env and `os.ModeCharDevice` on stdout; padding is applied to plain text first so colors never misalign columns

USER: Reviewer feedback (round 4). Now the architecture must be right:
1. Dependency injection everywhere: core logic must NOT call the filesystem or clock directly. Inject a Store interface (load/save), a Clock, and an output io.Writer; wire real impls only in main().
2. Pure core / thin shell: command logic operates on an in-memory task-list value; I/O at the edges.
3. Tests: table-driven, using an in-memory Store + a fake Clock (no real files / wall-clock in unit tests).
4. Use sentinel errors (ErrNotFound) wrapped with %w. No global mutable state; construct the app from its deps. Stdlib-only (no third-party deps); hand-rolled subcommand dispatch.
